english: Isgarren is higher in the tower. Dagda, a portal? I'm still collecting my strength...

Isgarren, Couldn't find you this morning for a chat, so I left this brief of what I wanted to discuss in your robe pocket. Waiting Dawn mentioned you were accompanying the wayfinder to more tropical surrounds. She was glad to have a chance to spend time in Amnytas, after all this time. I wanted to get her to jot down some notes on the Heart of the Obscure while she's here, but Dagda asked I not disturb her while she communes with the World Spire. Once again, trying to extract straightforward answers from you elder wizards is like trying to pry a diamond from a skritt's hands. Meanwhile, the Tyrian Alliance continues to drag their heels on our proposal to further assess activity in the Realm of Torment after Kormir's exodus and other recent events. My own digging during the titan mess turned up nothing new. Lucky for us, your expedition into Bava Nisos gave you a chance to assess the foundry, albeit from a distance. Maybe one day the alliance council will see the benefit of a more thorough investigation. For now, I've been fielding the occasional request from Ogden Stonehealer since our talks with the priory began. He and some of the other Deldrimor would like to talk, and I'm of two minds about it—figuratively speaking. You know my facets had some nasty bouts with Kryptis possession while you were wrestling with Cerus. Some of my suppressed memories were dredged to the surface, and well, let's just say my chances of a family reunion are pretty damn slim. Still, with dwarven memories being what they are, who can say if someone's kept a lantern lit for me, you know? Enough about my own baggage. Enjoy yourself! Let some of the world's weight off those world-bearing shoulders. Safe travels,  Lyhr

Isgarren,    I have approved a shipment of necessary supplies to help the lowlanders in their restoration efforts. The titans have devastated much of their local agriculture and infrastructure. We will be utilizing the portal that connects Divinity's Reach to Moon Camp to help deliver them in a timely manner. Countess Anise will provide you and Warden Frode with any details you may need.    I strongly advise you to cooperate, for the sake of our developing bonds with the Astral Ward.    —Queen Jennah of Kryta

Isgarren,    I have some good news, and I have some odd news.    The good news: we have confirmed the absence of any further titan activity on the scale of Ura, Greer, and Decima across Janthir Syntri and Lowland Shore.    The odd news: this has not fixed anything.    Lest you misunderstand, the situation is no longer deteriorating. We've seen some improvement in the vitality of regional flora and fauna. Narcisse informs me the salmon are "floppier than ever." But, given all we know, I would expect rather more recovery progress...and far fewer titanspawn.    The titans' defeat at the hand of the wayfinder should have brought about a more robust shift in the environment. After injury or illness, the body rights itself once more to equilibrium. However, our readings do not support projections of an upward recovery trend in Janthiri wildlife (and my researchers assure me it is not a byproduct of faulty equipment this time). Scouts continue to report titanspawn sightings across the isles, and Mistburned populations remain troublingly steady in number.    In a word: enervation. If I were to guess, I'd say vital energies required for the land to recover are being drained or redirected, somehow. A side effect of the bloodstone fallout in Syntri? It does seem unlikely based on what we know, but nothing can be ruled out where nasty little cultists are involved.    We shall continue to, as they say, "hold down the fort" in your absence as we endeavor to uncover the root of this quandary.    Though, knowing the wayfinder, I suspect whatever's causing it will find you first.    Enjoy the haunted ruins!    —Zizel

Isgarren,    I was initially nervous to stand in for you at alliance proceedings—there aren't many stoneless dwarves in Tyria, something that might encourage a slurry of personal questions. I suspect word will make it back to my people, now. A sobering thought, given I haven't been among them since just before the first dwarves turned, before I was picked up by the ward. But I digress, you and Dagda can recall those days better than I can.    Nostalgic musings aside; my first couple of days have gone well. The leaders and delegates are a lively bunch! There were times I thought I might split from some of the more heated discussions, but cooler heads seemed to prevail—I'm in my element. Currently, we're in talks concerning the quarantine and cleanup of Rata Primus in Dajkah. Councilor Imann and their Olmakhan neighbors are hoping to rally Arcane Council support, and Ludo seems optimistic. I suggest the ward offer additional support in this; if there's anything left in those ruins in need of containment, we are well equipped for that sort of thing. Of course, I'll wait for your response.    Last point of order, while I have you: I shouldn't have to remind you of all people that when you're here, you represent both the court and the Astral Ward. Allying with the Countess Exemplar was a shrewd choice, but remember, there are others here worthy of your time and attention, even if you don't see the immediate benefits.    Be seeing you,  —Lyhr

Isgarren—    Here are the books and artifacts acquired from Zakiros, as you requested. Kindly return these to the vault in a timely fashion—even wizards are not exempt from due dates, much as it seems they believe otherwise.    —Vivienne
It appears they're making their way to Lion's Arch. Provided this tracking enchantment doesn't wear out, I'll continue to provide updates on their whereabouts.—Commander Landon
It appears to be a large mace with a heavily corroded head, but whether it was practical or ceremonial isn't readily apparent.
It appears you have some unusable items. I can fix that for you.

It ate, and ate, and ate, and ateAnd gained a heap of dolyak weight.Dolyaks are tender when round and fat.Every Elonian hydra agrees with that.When the caravan was ready to goThe very full dolyak went much too slow.
It could be worth pursuing rumors of lost wreckage in the waters surrounding Seitung Province.
It did not feel good chasing a karka back to a Cavern where there could have been more dangers lying in wait.

It has been an unseasonably cold winter, yet seeing the lifting of this frosty brume brings a smile to my face.Birds, like all things in spring, are finally free to venture northward.Let us learn from their example.M.

It has been two sunsets since I last wrote my thoughts down. I have completed my pilgrimage and finally stand before the Spire of Exaltation. As I stand upon the ramp leading into the Temple of the Blessed Mantle, looking out across the waters running through the majestic landscape of Eternity's Garden, I am reminded of how this region has always held such mystery among our people.    I remember the stories my forebear would tell of his own journey to the temple. He never discussed his trials among our kith, but he told countless stories of the treacherous journey through the depths of the jungle to get there. It took him eight days along the pilgrim's path from the far southern reaches of Castora to the steps of the temple, and several of his cohort did not complete the journey.    My own pilgrimage ended fifteen days after I first set out on the path. Mentor Sidony reminds me that it is not a race, that each must come to Divitium Nava in their own time and in their own way.    Despite those reassurances, I am certain my forebear will judge the extra time I required. That is, if I pass the trials, which I can begin as soon as tomorrow.    I predict this will be another restless night.
It hits like a mountain and burns like a volcano. Steel yourself for the Megadestroyer!

It is a mistake that many make to say that hunters are damaging nature. After all, are we not part of nature? When we tracked a deer by endurance alone, claiming it only when exhaustion overtook it, with nothing but our bare hands, were we not part of nature then? Why then would a bow of wood and animal sinew make us unnatural?    The hunt is not about the desecration of nature but the celebration of our history and our traditions. So long as we do not overhunt or act cruelly in taking our prey, we are not doing a dishonorable thing. We are a part of the same cycle as our prey, and it's best we never forget that.

It is difficult to obtain intact mechs that have been verifiably corrupted by and purged of jade sparks, as Xunlai Jade does not keep records of devices after returning them to service. The samples here are under observation for lingering effects that would be undetectable without the Astral Ward's specialized equipment.
It is done. Komalie has opened. Was any of it worth it?    Some prepare for battle. I will wait for death.

It is done.We have purged this place of unbelievers. Every last one of the unfaithful...gone. Only three of us remain now... Ulrich, Judge, and I. It was not easy... Some of those among the fallen were friends. Once.But they refused our vision. They wished to give up on our chosen mission. To doubt the undying will of Caudecus, even after the noble sacrifice he made for us. They learned the penalty for such abhorrent betrayal. For disloyalty. And now, you who reads of our cause know the price of misgiving.With this journal, I leave behind the badge earned by passing my test, along with the others in my cohort of initiates. To accompany it...another surprise, should any of our former allies try to find us in this place we once considered sanctuary. The old ways are dead. We three shall now go forth to chart the future of the White Mantle. Upon our return, you will know the price for that future has been paid in our blood. We will come with cleansing fire.You have been warned.
It is I, your herald. Come meet me in the Domain of Kourna and we shall speak on your greatness.

It is my sad duty to inform you that, despite your heroic actions during the recent defense of Smokestead, your warband was overwhelmed and suffered catastrophic losses to personnel. Therefore, I am ordering you back to the Black Citadel immediately to discuss your next assignment. —Tribune Bhuer Goreblade
It is said that when Dwayna woke each morning, she gifted her followers with the warmth of dawn. All of the creatures in Tyria stirred from their slumber to witness the glorious sunrise.
It is shielding the tentacle from harm. Defeat it to continue your assault.
It is too soon to use your portal stone. You can use it again in %num1% seconds.

It is with a disquieted heart that I declare a new battle's eve is upon us. Few moons have passed since we last gathered for such an occassion. While our prior bravery and sacrifice were repaid in victory, I'm afraid we've no time to rest on our laurels.    We do, however, have time enough to feast before we heed battle's call. And so, I invite you to join our bearkin for an eve of food, drink, and dance. Let us prepare for tomorrow by celebrating the joys of today.    Your humbled Claw,  —Stoic Alder

It's a mystery that the Sons of Svanir managed to invade the Mists. Raven Havroun Weibe might have some insight into how and why this happened. He was last seen searching for a lost idol in the dredge caverns near Doldenvan Passage.

It's absorbing the energy of its fallen comrade. Its power rating is off the charts. Be careful.

It's been a relief to have something to keep track of tales and songs I've heard over the years. Easier to recite or riff on their original lines than to trust it to memory all the time, not to mention working on my own original pieces. --- From Orrian shores to Maguuma's coast,  Down fiery path and saga of frost,  O'er seas of jade at world's unend,  A cycle reborn, a world to mend.  *Notes: It's fine, but the connection to the Second Scion isn't as strong as I'd like. Maybe someone closer to Aurene can get it right?*--- ~~Her~~ fangs ~~were~~ out, ~~her~~ blade ~~was~~ sharp  Vengeance stirred within her heart  Oaths and lessons, never forsaken  ~~The Soulkeeper's~~ Her Vigil ~~forever~~ unshaken  *Notes: I picked this one up while visiting a friend at Vigil Keep. One of the norn warmasters was rather passionately reciting it from the commissary. From what I've heard, the lyrics of the original song were shorter and punchier, so I've cut back some the excess to honor the original form.* --- Alas poor Dougal, down on his luck.  An ancient charr relic, a deal was struck.  Friends and foes fell, an ally's oath spurned.  Claw in Keane hands, the addled king yearned.  Peace forged of doom, its ember yet burns.  *Notes: This one may be my favorite. It could be abbreviated further, but sometimes it's nice to let a tale flourish a little extra. Funny enough, I heard this one most in Ascalon. I wonder if Dougal knows that hulking feline soldiers and engineers gleefully chant his daring deeds?* --- Scholarly Firstborn, a burden he bore.  Three factions rallied—a Pact bound for Orr.  Armed with insight and Riannoc's blade.  To artesian waters his Wyld Hunt bade.  Life blooms anew, a legacy made.  *Notes: Trahearne's passing came as a blow to us, following the terrors of Mordremoth. I was heartened to hear many tales of him afterward that focused on his greatest triumph rather than the tragedy that befell him.* --- "That's my cue!" the mad sylvari cooed.   With clockwork knights she struck,  Those on her list. What luck!  "Begone you little pest," she said.  "Desist, my dears, or you'll be dead."  *Notes: I admit, I was baffled to hear that something had been written about Scarlet Briar, of all people. Some dubious bard in Divinity's Reach must have appreciated her flair for the dramatic? I guess if there were any Secondborn to write about, she'd be the one.* --- ~~Gathering~~ Council held, ~~in~~ times most dire.  Subtle as shadow, tempting ~~their~~ ire.  ~~A~~ Barrier ~~she~~ raised, against Mantle plot  ~~To claim~~ For Kryta's throne, ~~but all was for naught.~~  ~~Her allies struck true.~~ May Caudecus rot!  *Notes: Picked this one up in the promenade outside the queen's palace. I've made some revisions of my own, but I feel like someone who knows Her Majesty could punch this up just a bit more and give it a personal touch.* *Still, it would be an honor to perform this for her one day. Think of the exposure!*
It's been awhile since you checked in with Captain Logan Thackeray. The Seraph are always busy, and there's almost certainly something you could do to help out.
It's fascinating here, isn't it? We have an unprecedented opportunity to study the magic that was released.
It's filled to the brim with gold; looks like there is a note nearby as well.

It's finally over. We've reached a settlement, and I will not have to spend one more minute staring at Chul-Moo's miserable tanuki face. I hear his little side project is now calling themselves "the Jade Brotherhood." I used to think he was such a brilliant businessman, but now I see him for what he really is—a small-minded man with an even smaller bag of tricks. Acquiring the Xunlai brand was the last good idea he had for the company. I'm not so sure taking on the name of a bunch of long-dead mobsters will be quite as effective, but what do I know? I am, after all, a "visionless husk of a woman with a stick lodged so high up my rear end, it's a wonder I can bend at the waist."It almost didn't happen. At the last moment, his miserable mother got involved, insisting that she too was owed a payout for her "financial contributions to the company's early prototypes." Of course, Matron Park didn't anticipate my extensive recordkeeping—the look on her face when I produced the exact card in which she clearly referred to her contributions as a gift "for the future of my beautiful boy." I will treasure the memory.Gi-Moon and I ate so much janchi-guksu in celebration. He fixed it just the way Mother used to. Nothing has ever tasted so good.

It's going to be rough, but you've got the quaggan crab ranch off to a great start.
It's good to see Captain Thackeray on his feet. I thought he was a goner for sure.
It's hard to tell how many bandits use the cave hidden behind the cemetery, but the path leading to it shows a lot of wear. They probably won't notice one more new face, but you'll need a disguise.

It's nice to know that there are still people willing to help complete strangers. Because of your invaluable aid, I can continue my research into the tomb of Saul D'Alessio. I'm positive that what I find in there will make Tyria a better place.—Durmand Priory Explorer Ket

It's plain to see you have both the knack and the passion for it now—I'm glad our paths crossed. Whatever trouble may find you, remember to cast a line from time to time and find your center.Verse, well, you'll have to learn that on your own. An old man has to keep some secrets, eh?

It's time to rally to the defense of Southsun Cove. Canach is safely in Lionguard custody, but his dangerous scheme is still in motion. In a misguided effort to help people, Canach made the local wildlife a danger to every settler, tourist, and Consortium employee on the island. The Lionguard needs as many brave souls as it can muster to defend the lives of Southsun Cove's residents. If you've got the nerve and the skills, join us in showing Canach how real heroes help people.—Inspector Ellen Kiel
It's time to settle things with the mysterious Tactician Syska and clear your name after the tragic friendly fire incident that cost Pact soldiers their lives.

It's time to settle things with Two-Blade Pete once and for all. The streets of Divinity's Reach are far too dangerous with him on the loose. Even with Logan Thackeray's help, it won't be easy to lure Pete out and bring him to justice.

It's time you lazy lot learned the true meaning of combat. Attend my hand-to-hand crash course and shed your weakness.    Participate any evening at the Bastion of Strength.    *Note: By attending, you are acknowledging that Galrath is not responsible for any deaths or permanent injuries that may result from the training.*

It's warmer in Lion's Arch than it was in Gavril. I've been spending a lot more time outside here, so that makes me happy.    Whenever I feel my heart racing and I can't calm it, I walk to the lighthouse and sit for a while. On a bad night, the only thing that keeps my head from bursting is staring out at the water and counting the ships in the bay... It helps a little—there's a lone one out there right now. It'll be busy again in the morning. Trading day. I like it when the city is busy.    The innkeeper is kind. She's letting me use a small guest room in the back. Just a bed and a trunk, but that's all I need, really. I only had moments to escape, so I left with my dress and coat and some cheese stuffed in my pockets. I've returned the favor by baking bread for the tavern, just like I did at home. It reminds me of Mother... That's bittersweet.    She's gentle—the innkeeper. I can tell that she knows I struggled. She's curious, but she doesn't pry... I don't know if I'll ever be able to talk about it. All I know is that whenever one of those terrors from the White Mantle shows their face in town, mine turns white. But who could I tell? Who would believe me?    At least I escaped... I may be the only one.    Dorothy
